verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38) is a single-pass type I TM protein that resides predominantly in the trans-Golgi network but cycles continuously between the TGN and the plasma membrane via secretory vesicles. A minor but documented PM pool exists at steady state, with its ectodomain exposed extracellularly during the surface phase before rapid endocytosis back to the TGN. This dual-localization cycling qualifies for contextual surface accessibility.
